metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1821
metadata.comment:
Rationale: In figure 5, the authors use hippocampal neurons transfected with shRNAs targeting AP-1 (I checked the methods section and although they are never really clear, a blast shows that the shRNA targets the sigma subunit of AP-1. They then stimulate with 300 APs at 10 Hz (this will drive bulk endocytosis). AP-1 (sigma 1) knockdown decreases endosome driven bulk endocytosis.

On the invesigated protein:
Double-checking the annotated protein, I find that the AP1 protein studied in this paper is most likely AP1-gamma (and not AP1-sigma).

From M&M:
"The shRNA for AP-1 γ subunit (rat), AP-3 δ subunit (rat) were designed from following oligonucleotides. shRNA AP-1, 5'-GCGCCTGTACAAGGCAATT-3';"

nucleotide BLAST analysis of this shRNA confirms this is AP1G1: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/NM_134460.1

( you can manually confirm the "GCGCCTGTACAAGGCAATT" shRNA match @ https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/NM_134460.1?report=fasta )

13/11/2017 Pim
- I manually validated the BLAST search above. No other rat AP1 subunits match this shRNA sequence.. Experimental description: From the methods, "Hippocampal neurons derived from E-18 pregnant Sprague Dawley female rats...".
